query: Mg2+ ion doping and Li1.3La0.3Ti1.7(PO4)3 surface coating is coupled with an explicit mechanism-stress intervention in L...[truncated] If Mg2+ ion doping and Li1.3La0.3Ti1.7(PO4)3 surface coating is coupled with an explicit mechanism-stress intervention in Li-rich Mn-based oxide cathodes in all-solid-state lithium batteries, then capacity retention will reveal a hidden boundary condition behind the gap: Potential conclusion conflict: overlapping claim context contains opposing claims: Full text excerpt: Lattice-matched LiCoPO4 coating enable...[truncated] This proposal addresses the PaperGraph gap 'Potential conclusion conflict: overlapping claim context contains opposing claims: Full text excerpt: Lattice-matched LiCoPO4 coating enables stable cycling of LiCoO2 at 4.6–4.7 V by stabilizing the cathode–electrolyte interface, suppressing oxygen release, and m...[truncated] vs Especially at a high voltage above 4.5 V, LiCoO2 faces severe challenges, such as irreversible phase transition, lattice ox...[truncated] Increasing the operating voltage can signi...[truncated].'. It hypothesizes a testable mechanism: Concrete mechanism: Mg2+ ion doping and Li1.3La0.3Ti1.7(PO4)3 surface coating contributes through a specified operation that must be mapped to observable inputs and outputs before validation. In Li-rich Mn-based oxide cathodes in all-solid-state lithium batteries, the specific target process is the scenario-specific measurable process named by the project evidence. The method's input requirements are broad or not clearly specified; the experiment must make them explicit. No explicit bridge concept is currently visible; this must be treated as a human-review assumption rather than a validated mechanism. The decisive prediction is that this concrete bridge, not a generic representation change, will alter capacity retention; if the bridge data or causal link is absent, the hypothesis should fail rather than be reinterpreted post hoc. MingLi treats this as a structural mutation rather than a surface rephrasing. The study is designed to be falsifiable through Build a minimal benchmark for Li-rich Mn-based oxide cathodes in all-solid-state lithium batteries; compare Mg2+ ion doping and Li1.3La0.3Ti1.7(PO4)3 surface coating against canonical baselines; measure capacity retention; include negative controls, ablations, and failure-mode analysis. Grounding evidence comes from: Xuerui Yang et al. (2022) Pushing Lithium Cobalt Oxides to 4.7 V by Lattice‐Matched Interfacial Engineering doi:10.1002/aenm.202200197, Gaoqiang Mao et al. (2025) Optimizing the electrochemical performance of LiCoO2 at 4.5 V via synergistic modification of Mg2+ ion doping and Li1.3La0.3Ti1.7(PO4)3 coating doi:10.1007/s42114-025-01373-3. The proposal differs by testing the mapped gap directly with explicit baselines, ablations, and failure-mode criteria.
